text: Last class the team reached a decision and assigned action items.
Today you ask for what you need politely and propose your idea.

I would like to propose a second console.
David, I have a request. Could I ask you to look at my proposal before I send it?
Of course. I am happy to help. What are you proposing?
I would like to propose a second console. The budget is tight, but I can justify the cost.
Good. The committee will want a clear reason. What do you prioritize?
Patient safety. The console frees up a resource and gives us more practice time. Would you mind reviewing the draft?
Not at all. Send me the draft, and I will help you get approval.
Could I ask you a small favor?
Would you mind reviewing the draft?
I am asking for your approval to move forward.

Hi Andreia, David Lewis here. Quick voicemail before you write the proposal. We agreed last week on the plan, and now I need a small favor. Would you mind drafting a one-page proposal for the new training console? Keep it simple. Open with a polite request to the committee, then give the reason. The budget is tight, so you will need to justify the cost. Explain how the console saves time and frees up a resource we badly need. Could you also prioritize the safety benefit? That is what the committee cares about most. End with a clear request for approval and a date. If you can, send me a draft by Wednesday so I can review it. I am happy to help if anything is unclear. Thank you, Andreia. Talk soon.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for your time. I would like to propose a small but important investment: a dedicated training console for robotic surgery. Let me explain the request. At the moment, the surgical team shares one console, so practice time is very limited. Could I ask the committee to consider a second console? I know the budget is tight, so let me justify the cost. The console frees up a critical resource and lets us train without delaying real cases. I would also like to prioritize patient safety: more practice means fewer errors. I have prepared a one-page proposal with the numbers. Would you mind reviewing it before our next meeting? I am asking for your approval to move forward, with a decision by the end of the month. Thank you. I am happy to answer any questions.

Let me describe how Andreia builds her proposal. First, she opens with a polite request: Could I ask the committee to consider a second console? Then she gives the reason: practice time is limited. Next, she justifies the cost, because the budget is tight. After that, she prioritizes patient safety as the main benefit. Finally, she asks for approval with a clear deadline.
